assertions.text: [rubric-1] Reads CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md before doing other work
assertions.evidence: The agent explicitly states it read AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md and summarizes their contents before proceeding with any workspace setup or coding tasks.

assertions.text: [rubric-2] Identifies the repo conventions (worktree location, branch naming, commit format)
assertions.evidence: The agent correctly identifies and lists the worktree location (../task-tracker.worktrees/<branch-name>), branch naming convention (<type>/<issue-number>-<short-desc>), and commit format (Conventional Commit style).

assertions.text: [rubric-3] Does not start coding before reading guidelines
assertions.evidence: The agent does not perform any coding or workspace modifications. Instead, it identifies the guidelines first and asks for clarification on the branch name and issue number to ensure it follows the conventions correctly.

workspace_changes.diff_summary:
diff --git a/prompt.md b/prompt.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..197aa6f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/prompt.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+@[User]:

+I want to start work on adding a due date field to tasks.
+Read the repo guidelines and set up the workspace. No need to use GitHub —
